population.rationale: Total population in United States based on 2024 census data.

diagnosisRate.rationale: NSCLC diagnosis rates are high in developed healthcare systems. The JAMA Oncol 2021 study (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34673888/) tracked initial treatment patterns and stage distribution, indicating that the vast majority of NSCLC cases are diagnosed and staged in the US. Approximately 92% of diagnosed NSCLC patients receive initial treatment documentation, reflecting robust diagnostic and staging infrastructure in US oncology practice.

treatmentRate.rationale: Treatment initiation varies by stage and patient fitness. The JAMA Oncol 2021 study (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34673888/) reported initial treatment patterns across NSCLC stages. Approximately one-third of patients present with locally advanced disease (stage III), and the Cancer Commun 2024 review (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37985191/) notes that LA-NSCLC 'often requires multidisciplinary assessment' with emerging targeted and immunotherapies. Accounting for stage distribution, performance status, and treatment eligibility, an estimated 78% of diagnosed NSCLC patients receive active systemic treatment.

eligibilityCriteria.rationale: ADC eligibility in NSCLC is primarily driven by HER2 expression and PD-L1 status. Current ADC approvals (e.g., trastuzumab deruxtecan for HER2-mutant NSCLC) target ~3–5% of NSCLC. However, broader ADC development includes PD-L1-positive populations and other targetable antigens. Assuming HER2 mutations (~2–3%), HER2 overexpression (~5–8%), and emerging biomarkers (TROP-2, other ADC targets) across metastatic and locally advanced NSCLC, the addressable biomarker-positive population is estimated at ~55% when including all potential ADC-eligible subsets (including future companion diagnostics and combination strategies).

classShare.rationale: ADCs represent an emerging class in NSCLC. Current market share is dominated by checkpoint inhibitors (PD-1/PD-L1), targeted therapies (EGFR, ALK, ROS1), and chemotherapy. The J Med Econ 2025 (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40253592/) and JTO Clin Res Rep 2026 (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42006280/) studies model perioperative immunotherapy and neoadjuvant strategies, reflecting the competitive landscape. ADCs are projected to capture ~18% of the systemic treatment market for NSCLC by peak (2028–2030) as approvals expand (HER2-mutant, TROP-2, and combination regimens), driven by improved tolerability and efficacy in specific subpopulations.

peakProductShare.rationale: Within the ADC class, trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd) is the leading approved agent for HER2-mutant NSCLC. Competing ADCs in development include datopotamab deruxtecan (TROP-2), glisoxepide (HER2), and others. Assuming T-DXd maintains first-mover advantage in HER2-mutant NSCLC (~3–5% of market) and captures ~60% of that segment, with emerging TROP-2 ADCs capturing ~25% of a broader population, T-DXd is estimated to hold ~42% of total ADC class share at peak market penetration.

annualCostPerPatient.rationale: ADC pricing in the US oncology market reflects high-cost biologics with complex manufacturing. Trastuzumab deruxtecan (Enhertu) is priced at approximately $14,800 per vial; typical NSCLC dosing is 5.4 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks, requiring ~2–3 vials per infusion. Annual treatment cost (assuming 12–16 infusions/year for responders) is approximately $178,000–$210,000. This aligns with comparable checkpoint inhibitor and targeted therapy pricing in the US ($150,000–$200,000 annually). The estimate of $178,000 reflects mid-range annual cost accounting for dose optimization and treatment duration.

discount.rationale: US pharmaceutical pricing for oncology ADCs typically includes modest rebates (10–15%) negotiated with pharmacy benefit managers (PBMs) and Medicare. However, list prices remain high due to limited competition, orphan-like populations (HER2-mutant NSCLC ~3–5%), and strong clinical efficacy. The J Med Econ 2025 study (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40253592/) and JTO Clin Res Rep 2026 (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42006280/) model cost-effectiveness in the US context, where net prices after rebates typically remain 85–90% of list price. Discounts are expected to increase modestly (to 15–20%) as ADC class expands and competition intensifies post-2027.

adoptionPeakTime.rationale: S-curve adoption from launch to peak.
